address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

summary.p: 1. Several MS. Persian taşnifs or ballads collected and sent to Professor Browne by George Grahame, then H.B.M. Consul at Shíráz, in October, 1905.

2. Lithographed Muharram poems (ta'ziyas and rawḍa-khadas), including the following six ta'ziyas: (1) Kitáb-i Durrat al-Ṣadaf; (2) Shahádat-i Hurr ibn Yazíd Riyáhí; (3) Kitáb-i Shahádat-i Ḥaḍrat-i Sayyid Sajjád u Imám Zaynu'l-'Ábidín; (4) Majlis-i Amír Tímúr bá Wafát-i Zaynab Khátún; (5) Majlis-i Ta'ziyya-i Khurúj Namúdan-i 'Abdu'lláh Yazdgird bá Lashkar-i Yárí-yi Sayyidu'sh-Shuhadá u Ámadan ba-Dasht-i Karbalá; (6) Majlis-i Shahádat-i Imám.

3. A MS. Bábí poem by Mírzá Na'ím of Ábádé (see A Year amongst the Persians, p. 567 of the reprint), received from G. Grahame in August, 1902, with a letter in which the writer says that Mírzá Muḥammad or Mírzá Na'ím really came from Sidih.
4. A MS. Kurdish poem received from the Rev. W. St Clair Tisdall in 1912.

5. MS. poems by Mírzá Dáwarí of Shíráz, a son of the poet known as Wiṣál (see A Year amongst the Persians, reprint, pp. 130 and 292). They were given to Professor Browne in the winter of 1887-8 at Ṭihrán by the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan-'Alí Khán.

6. A qaṣída composed in 1887 for Queen Victoria's Jubilee by Mírzá-yi Farhang of Shíráz, a younger brother of the above-mentioned Mírzá Dáwarí (see A Year amongst the Persians, loc. cit.), and copied by Professor Browne from the original belonging to the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan-'Alí Khán. It is followed by a ghazal composed in honour of Professor Browne by Hájjí Pír-záda.
7. Bábí MS. tracts given to Professor Browne by Basil Williams, Feb. 7, 1897, comprising (a) Risála-i-Siyásiyya; (b) Lawh-i Bishárát; (c) Lawh-i Zardishtiyán; (d) Munáját.
8. Professor Browne's pencil copy, made in Cambridge, Nov. 4, 1900, of the text of a "Tablet" revealed by "the Most Great Branch" (Abbás Efendi) to Mr A. P. Dodge.
9. MS. commentary by 'Abbás Efendi on the Hadith Kuntu Kanzan, "I was a Hidden Treasure," written for 'Ali Shevket Páshá and obtained from Áqá Jawád the dallál in Isfahán on March 1, 1888.

10. A short life of the famous philosopher Hájjí Mullá Hádí of Sabzawár composed by his pupil Mírzá Asadu'lláh of Sabzawár in Ṭihrán, February, 1888. This is the memoir from which Professor Browne derived the account of Hájjí Mullá Hádí given in A Year amongst the Persians. See the reprint, p. 146, note 1.

11. A cutting from the Pall Mall Gazette, Nov. 26, 1891, containing a letter by Professor Browne on the persecution of the Bábís in Yazd in May, 1891, together with the original rough draft of a MS. Persian translation of the same made for Náşiru'd-Dín Sháh by Mírzá Husayn-qulí Khán.
12. An Arabic tract, mystical in character, composed by Maḥmúd ibn Muḥammad at-Tabrízí in 1255/1839-40 and dedicated to Muḥammad Sháh Qájár. The lithograph bears the date 1264/1847-8. Begins:
الحمد لله الذي هدانا شرايع الاحكام

msItem.title: Several MS. Persian taşnífs (ballads)
msItem.note: Collected and sent to Browne by George Grahame, then H.B.M. Consul at Shíráz, in October, 1905.

msItem.title: Six lithographed Muḥarram poems (ta'ziyas and rawḍa-khadas)
Bábí poem
msItem.note: See A Year amongst the Persians, p. 567 of the reprint. Received from G. Grahame in August, 1902; Grahame's covering letter states that Mírzá Muḥammad, or Mírzá Na'ím, in fact came from Sidih.

msItem.title: Kurdish poem
msItem.note: Received from the Rev. W. St Clair Tisdall in 1912.
given to Browne in the winter of 1887-8 at Țihrán by the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan- Alí Khán.

msItem.title: Poems
msItem.note: Son of the poet known as Wiṣál; see A Year amongst the Persians, reprint, pp. 130 and 292. Given to Browne in the winter of 1887-8 at Ṭihrán by the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan-'Alí Khán.

msItem.title: Qaṣída for Queen Victoria's Jubilee, followed by a ghazal in honour of Professor Browne by Hájjí Pír-záda
msItem.note: Mírzá-yi Farhang was a younger brother of Mírzá Dáwarí (item 5). Composed 1887; copied by Browne from the original belonging to the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan-'Alí Khán.
Given to Browne by Basil Williams, Feb. 7, 1897.

msItem.title: Browne's pencil copy of the text of a "Tablet" revealed by "the Most Great Branch" ('Abbás Efendi) to Mr A. P. Dodge
msItem.note: Copied by Browne in Cambridge, Nov. 4, 1900.

msItem.title: Commentary on the Hadith Kuntu Kanzan ("I was a Hidden Treasure")
msItem.note: Written for 'Alí Shevket Páshá; obtained from Áqá Jawád the dallál in Isfahán on March 1, 1888.

msItem.title: Short life of Hájjí Mullá Hádí of Sabzawár
msItem.note: Composed by his pupil Mírzá Asadu'lláh of Sabzawár in Ṭihrán, February, 1888. Source of Browne's account of Hájjí Mullá Hádí (Sabzavārī, Hādī ibn Mahdī, 1797 or 1798-1873) in A Year amongst the Persians (reprint, p. 146, note 1).

msItem.title: Pall Mall Gazette cutting and draft Persian translation
msItem.note: A cutting from the Pall Mall Gazette, Nov. 26, 1891, containing a letter by Browne on the persecution of the Bábís at Yazd in May, 1891, together with the original rough draft of a Persian translation of the same made for Náşiru'd-Dín Sháh by Mírzá Husayn-qulí Khán.

msItem.title: Mystical Arabic tract
msItem.note: Composed in 1255/1839-40, dedicated to Muḥammad Sháh Qájár; the lithograph bears the date 1264/1847-8.
msItem.incipit: الحمد لله الذي هدانا شرايع الاحكام

supportDesc.support: Paper; a bound miscellany of manuscript, lithographed and printed items of varying format (loose tracts, a newspaper cutting, pencil copies), not a single continuously-paginated text.
supportDesc.extent: 12 items as listed; individual extents and dimensions not given by Browne for this volume as a whole.

layout.p: Not applicable to the volume as a whole.

handDesc.handNote: Numerous hands, including Professor Browne's own (items 6 and 8).

history.provenance: A miscellany assembled by Browne from items received from several different sources between 1887 and 1912: George Grahame (items 1 and 3), the Rev. W. St Clair Tisdall (item 4), the Nawwáb Mírzá Hasan-'Alí Khán (items 5 and 6), Basil Williams (1867-1950) (item 7), and Browne's own copying (items 6, 8 and 11).
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: Summary, physical description and provenance copied from R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions. Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Claude (auto-upgrade) Auto-upgraded on the model of D_13.xml: built 12 msItems from the numbered "Contents" list in the summary (repositioning a stray "5." that had been printed before "4." in the source); added script="unknown" (hands vary by item) and LCSH keywords. physDesc is necessarily approximate: this is a bound miscellany of unrelated items in mixed formats with no single extent/dimensions/line-count given by Browne. Author keys left blank throughout — none of Grahame, Na'ím, Dáwarí, Farhang, Hájjí Pír-záda, Basil Williams, 'Abbás Efendi, A. P. Dodge, Áqá Jawád, Hájjí Mullá Hádí, Mírzá Asadu'lláh, Náşiru'd-Dín Sháh, Mírzá Husayn-qulí Khán or Maḥmúd ibn Muḥammad at-Tabrízí has an existing Fihrist key in this corpus; reused person_27381460 for Rev. W. St Clair Tisdall. idno suffix "(8)" dropped as with D_2. Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
